name: AWS S3
description: Amazon Simple Storage Service — object storage. Bucket-scoped REST endpoints with AWS SigV4 request signing. Use put_object/get_object/list_objects/delete_object from any app needing durable cloud storage (backup, media, etc.).

credential_fields.name: access_key_id
credential_fields.label: AWS Access Key ID
credential_fields.description: IAM access key with s3:GetObject / s3:PutObject / s3:DeleteObject / s3:ListBucket on the buckets the app will use.

credential_fields.name: region
credential_fields.label: AWS Region
credential_fields.description: Region the bucket lives in (e.g. us-east-1, eu-west-1). Bucket-region mismatch produces a 301 redirect rather than a 4xx, so getting this right matters.

credential_fields.name: session_token
credential_fields.label: AWS Session Token
credential_fields.description: Only required when using temporary credentials issued by STS (assume-role, federated identities). Leave blank for long-lived IAM user keys.

tools.name: list_objects
tools.description: List objects in a bucket (S3 ListObjectsV2). Returns XML the caller parses; key fields are Contents[].Key, Contents[].Size, Contents[].LastModified, IsTruncated, NextContinuationToken.

prefix.description: Filter to keys starting with this prefix.

continuation-token.description: Pagination cursor from a prior IsTruncated response.

tools.name: put_object
tools.description: Upload an object. The request body is the object content verbatim; pass it as the 'body' input. Inputs are signed (SigV4) and the body is hashed for integrity.

body.description: Object content. Bytes welcome — the runner sends them verbatim.

tools.name: get_object
tools.description: Download an object. Response body is the raw object bytes.

tools.name: delete_object
tools.description: Delete a single object. Returns 204 No Content on success.

tools.name: list_buckets
tools.description: List all buckets owned by the authenticated account. Returns XML; ListAllMyBucketsResult.Buckets.Bucket[] has the names + creation dates.

tools.name: create_bucket
tools.description: Create an S3 bucket. The body field carries the optional CreateBucketConfiguration XML — leave it empty for us-east-1 (the default region for the API), and pass `<CreateBucketConfiguration xmlns="http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/"><LocationConstraint>REGION</LocationConstraint></CreateBucketConfiguration>` for any other region. Bucket names are globally unique, 3–63 chars, lowercase + DNS-safe. Returns 200 with empty body on success; 409 BucketAlreadyOwnedByYou is the idempotent-success case (treat as ok).

bucket.description: Bucket name. Must be globally unique, lowercase, 3–63 DNS-safe chars.

body.description: CreateBucketConfiguration XML for non-us-east-1 regions, or empty string for us-east-1.

tools.name: put_bucket_policy
tools.description: Attach (or replace) the bucket policy. Body is the policy as a JSON string — pass it via the `policy` input. Used to grant external AWS services (e.g. ses.amazonaws.com s3:PutObject) write access to the bucket. Returns 204 on success.

policy.description: Bucket policy as a JSON string. Example for SES inbound: {"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Sid":"AllowSESPuts","Effect":"Allow","Principal":{"Service":"ses.amazonaws.com"},"Action":"s3:PutObject","Resource":"arn:aws:s3:::BUCKET/*","Condition":{"StringEquals":{"AWS:SourceAccount":"ACCOUNT_ID"}}}]}

tools.name: get_bucket_policy
tools.description: Fetch the current bucket policy as a JSON string. Useful for idempotent bootstrap — read first, decide whether to upsert. Returns 404 NoSuchBucketPolicy when no policy is attached.
